revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:November 29 (old lunar calendar)]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of November 29 (old lunar calendar)]]</includeonly>

'''旧暦11月29日'''は[[旧暦]][[11月 (旧暦)|11月]]の29日目である。年によっては11月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#先負|先負]]である。
== できごと == * [[天元]]元年<!--（[[978年]]_月_日）--> - 翌年が陽五の厄に当たる為、天元に改元 * [[文治]]元年<!--（[[1185年]]_月_日）--> - [[鎌倉幕府]]が、[[源義経]]追討を名目に諸国に[[守護]]・[[地頭]]を置くことを[[後白河法皇]]に勅許させる
== 誕生日 == * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年<!--（[[1546年]]_月_日）--> - [[黒田孝高]]（如水・官兵衛）、[[武将]]（+ [[1604年]]） * [[明治]]3年<!--（[[1870年]]_月_日）--> - [[小金井喜美子]]、小説家・随筆家・[[森鴎外]]の妹（+ [[1956年]]）
== 忌日 ==
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
{{msg:旧暦360日}} *前日[[11月28日 (旧暦)|11月28日]] 翌日[[11月30日 (旧暦)|11月30日]] - 前月[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]] 翌月[[12月29日 (旧暦)|12月29日]] *新暦[[11月29日]]
{{msg:stub}}

== 誕生日 == * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年<!--（[[1546年]]_月_日）--> - [[黒田孝高]]（如水・官兵衛）、[[武将]]（+ [[1604年]]） * [[明治]]3年<!--（[[1870年]]_月_日）--> - [[小金井喜美子]]、小説家・随筆家・[[森鴎外]]の妹（+ [[1956年]]）
== 忌日 ==
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
{{旧暦360日}} *前日[[11月28日 (旧暦)|11月28日]] 翌日[[11月30日 (旧暦)|11月30日]] - 前月[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]] 翌月[[12月29日 (旧暦)|12月29日]] *新暦[[11月29日]]
{{stub}}

{{旧暦11月}} '''旧暦11月29日'''は[[旧暦]][[11月 (旧暦)|11月]]の29日目である。年によっては11月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#先負|先負]]である。
== できごと == * [[天元 (元号)|天元]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[978年]][[12月31日]]） - 翌年が陽五の厄に当たる為、天元に改元 * [[文治]]元年（ユリウス暦[[1185年]][[12月22日]]） - [[鎌倉幕府]]が、[[源義経]]追討を名目に諸国に[[守護]]・[[地頭]]を置くことを[[後白河法皇]]に勅許させる
== 誕生日 == * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（ユリウス暦[[1546年]][[12月22日]]） - [[黒田孝高]]（如水・官兵衛）、[[武将]]（+ [[1604年]]） * [[明治]]3年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1871年]][[1月19日]]） - [[小金井喜美子]]、[[小説家]]・[[随筆家]]・[[森鴎外]]の妹（+ [[1956年]]）
== 忌日 ==
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
{{旧暦360日}} *前日[[11月28日 (旧暦)|11月28日]] 翌日[[11月30日 (旧暦)|11月30日]] - 前月[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]] 翌月[[12月29日 (旧暦)|12月29日]] *新暦[[11月29日]]
{{stub}}

== 誕生日 == * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（ユリウス暦[[1546年]][[12月22日]]） - [[黒田孝高]]（如水・官兵衛）、[[武将]]（+ [[1604年]]） * [[明治]]3年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1871年]][[1月19日]]） - [[小金井喜美子]]、[[小説家]]・[[随筆家]]・[[森鴎外]]の妹（+ [[1956年]]）

[[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}}, then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}}, then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}}) – Because the following year would fall on the unlucky cycle of Yang Wu, the era was changed from {{ill|Jōgen|ja|貞元}} to {{ill|Tengen|ja|天元}}. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The Kamakura shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼経}}, was authorized by Emperor {{ill|Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河法皇}} to appoint governors (守護 and 地頭) in the provinces.
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}15年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} (Yosui or Kanbei), a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}}). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}3年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}}) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}}, a novelist, essayist, and the younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan (calculation expert) († circa {{ill|1660|ja|1660}}).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|28|11|30|10|29|12|11|1129}}

[[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}}, then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}}, then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}}) – Because the following year would fall on the unlucky cycle of Yang Wu, the era was changed from {{ill|Jōgen|ja|貞元}} to {{ill|Tengen|ja|天元}}. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The Kamakura shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼経}}, was authorized by Emperor {{ill|Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河法皇}} to appoint governors (守護 and 地頭) in the provinces.
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}15年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} (Yosui or Kanbei), a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}}). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}3年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}}) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}}, a novelist, essayist, and the younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan (calculation expert) († circa {{ill|1660|ja|1660}}).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|28|11|30|10|29|12|11|1129}}

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}19年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|747|ja|747}} {{ill|November 6|ja|11月6日}}) – The casting of the great Buddha at Tōdai-ji began. * {{ill|Kennin|ja|建仁}}3年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1203|ja|1203}} {{ill|November 4|ja|11月4日}}) – The Kamakura shogunate ordered Regent {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|ja|北条時政}} to confine Shogun {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼家}} to Izu (【Izu Shūzenji】). * {{ill|Tenwa (Japan)|ja|天和}}元年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1681|ja|1681}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) – Due to the Xinyou Revolution, the era name was changed from {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} to {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和}}.
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}15年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}}, a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}}). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}3年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}}) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}}, a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).

[[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}19年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|747|ja|747}} {{ill|November 6|ja|11月6日}}) – The casting of the great Buddha at Tōdai-ji began. * {{ill|Kennin|ja|建仁}}3年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1203|ja|1203}} {{ill|November 4|ja|11月4日}}) – The Kamakura shogunate ordered Regent {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|ja|北条時政}} to confine Shogun {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼家}} to Izu (【Izu Shūzenji】). * {{ill|Tenwa (Japan)|ja|天和}}1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1681|ja|1681}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) – Due to the Xinyou Revolution, the era name was changed from {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} to {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和}}.
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}15年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}}, a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}}). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}3年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}}) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}}, a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).

== Events == * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}19th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|747|ja|747}} {{ill|November 6|ja|11月6日}}) – The casting of the great Buddha at Tōdai-ji began. * {{ill|Kennin|ja|建仁}}3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1203|ja|1203}} {{ill|November 4|ja|11月4日}}) – The Kamakura shogunate ordered Regent {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|ja|北条時政}} to confine Shogun {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼家}} to Izu (【Izu Shūzenji】). * {{ill|Tenwa (Japan)|ja|天和}}1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1681|ja|1681}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) – Due to the Xinyou Revolution, the era name was changed from {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} to {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和}}.
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}}, a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}}). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}}) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}}, a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}}{{ill|978|ja|978年}}{{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}}) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}}{{ill|1185|ja|1185年}}{{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.

== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).

[[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}}{{ill|978|ja|978年}}{{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}}) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}}{{ill|1185|ja|1185年}}{{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}}, a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}}). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}}) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}}, a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}}{{ill|978|ja|978年}}{{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}}) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}}{{ill|1185|ja|1185年}}{{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}}, a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}}). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}}) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}}, a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

[[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}}{{ill|978|ja|978年}}{{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}}) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}}{{ill|1185|ja|1185年}}{{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}}, a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}}). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}}) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}}, a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|宝暦}}7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}}) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}}, a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

[[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] {{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978年}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185年}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

[[Category:Senbu]] [[Category:not Butsumetsus]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:Days 29 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:null category]] [[Category:U Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category: Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Lunisolar]] [[Category:Lunisolar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

[[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] {{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978年}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185年}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|" to "{{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|"
revision.text:
[[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978年}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185年}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

[[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978年}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185年}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978年}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185年}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .

[[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978年}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).

== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).

[[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunisolar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978th year }} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

[[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 29일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] [[zh:十一月廿九]] [[en:draft:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunisolar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|978|ja|978th year }} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭}} throughout the provinces.
[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]][[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:November 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:出来事]]
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660}} circa).

[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] {{Lunisolar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November|12=draft}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元|12=draft}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|978|ja|978th year|12=draft}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日|12=draft}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元|12=draft}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=draft}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=draft}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭|12=draft}} throughout the provinces.
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=draft}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外|12=draft}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki|12=draft}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758|12=draft}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=draft}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660|12=draft}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} and its day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元|12=draft}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|978|ja|978th year|12=draft}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日|12=draft}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元|12=draft}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=draft}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=draft}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭|12=draft}} throughout the provinces.
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=draft}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外|12=draft}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki|12=draft}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758|12=draft}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=draft}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660|12=draft}} circa).

[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] {{Lunisolar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November|12=draft}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元|12=draft}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|978|ja|978th year|12=draft}} {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日|12=draft}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Japan)|ja|貞元 (日本)|lt=Jōgen|lt_ja=貞元|12=draft}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=draft}} * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=draft}} to station {{ill|shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} and {{ill|jitō|ja|地頭|12=draft}} throughout the provinces.
== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=draft}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外|12=draft}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki|12=draft}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758|12=draft}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=draft}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660|12=draft}} circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129}}

== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=draft|12=draft}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外|12=draft|12=draft}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki|12=draft|12=draft}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=draft|12=draft}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660|12=draft|12=draft}} circa).

[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿九]] {{Lunisolar November}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November|12=draft|12=draft}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft|12=draft}}.

== Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Takahisa|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|ja|1604|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=draft|12=draft}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|ja|森鷗外|12=draft|12=draft}} († {{ill|1956|ja|1956|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|Hōreki|12=draft|12=draft}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1758|ja|1758|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|January 9|ja|1月9日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=draft|12=draft}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|ja|1660|12=draft|12=draft}} circa).

revision.text:
{{旧暦11月}} '''旧暦11月29日'''は[[11月 (旧暦)|旧暦11月]]の29日目である。年によっては11月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#先負|先負]]である。
== できごと == * [[天元 (日本)|天元]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[978年]][[12月31日]]） - 翌年が陽五の厄に当たるため[[貞元 (日本)|貞元]]より天元に[[改元]] * [[文治]]元年（ユリウス暦[[1185年]][[12月22日]]） - [[鎌倉幕府]]が、[[源義経]]追討を名目に諸国に[[守護]]・[[地頭]]を置くことを[[後白河天皇|後白河法皇]]に勅許させる
== 誕生日 == * [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（ユリウス暦[[1546年]][[12月22日]]） - [[黒田孝高]]（如水・官兵衛）、[[武将]]（+ [[1604年]]） * [[明治]]3年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1871年]][[1月19日]]） - [[小金井喜美子]]、[[小説家]]・[[随筆家]]・[[森鷗外]]の妹（+ [[1956年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[宝暦]]7年（ユリウス暦[[1758年]][[1月9日]]） - [[久留島喜内]]<ref>{{Cite Kotobank|word=久留島義太|encyclopedia=[[小学館]]デジタル大辞泉|accessdate=2021-02-26 }}</ref>（久留島義太）、[[和算家]]（+ [[1660年]]頃）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|11|28|11|30|10|29|12|11|1129}}
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿九]][[zh:十一月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q840521]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 29|qq=November 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|qid=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}. == Events == * [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:978|978]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]] ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Heian period)|Jōgen]] to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to station [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] and [[:en:Jitō|jitō]] throughout the provinces. == Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Takahisa]] , a military commander († [[:en:1604|1604]] ). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 19|January 19]] ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei Kimiko|qq=|1=Koganei Kimiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464316}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of [[:en:Mori Ōgai|Mori Ōgai]] († [[:en:1956|1956]] ).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 7th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1758|1758]] [[:en:January 9|January 9]] ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurushima Yoshinobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久留島義太|1=Kurushima Kinai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3915605}} , a wasan expert (* [[:en:1660|1660]] circa).

day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}. == Events == * [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:978|978]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]] ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Heian period)|Jōgen]] to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|lt=Go-Shirakawa ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 19|January 19]] ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei Kimiko|qq=|1=Koganei Kimiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464316}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of [[:en:Mori Ōgai|Mori Ōgai]] († [[:en:1956|1956]] ).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿九]][[zh:十一月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q840521]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 29|qq=November 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}. == Events == * [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:978|978]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]] ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Heian period)|Jōgen]] to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to station [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] and [[:en:Jitō|jitō]] throughout the provinces. == Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Takahisa]] , a military commander († [[:en:1604|1604]] ). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 19|January 19]] ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei Kimiko|1=Koganei Kimiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464316}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of [[:en:Mori Ōgai|Mori Ōgai]] († [[:en:1956|1956]] ).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 7th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1758|1758]] [[:en:January 9|January 9]] ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久留島義太|1=Kurushima Kinai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3915605}} , a wasan expert (* [[:en:1660|1660]] circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}. == Events == * [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:978|978]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]] ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Heian period)|Jōgen]] to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to station [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] and [[:en:Jitō|jitō]] throughout the provinces. == Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Takahisa]] , a military commander († [[:en:1604|1604]] ). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 19|January 19]] ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei Kimiko|1=Koganei Kimiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464316}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of [[:en:Mori Ōgai|Mori Ōgai]] († [[:en:1956|1956]] ).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 7th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1758|1758]] [[:en:January 9|January 9]] ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久留島義太|1=Kurushima Kinai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3915605}} , a wasan expert (* [[:en:1660|1660]] circa).

([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Takahisa]] , a military commander († [[:en:1604|1604]] ). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 19|January 19]] ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Heian period)|Jōgen]] to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing [[:en:Minamoto <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿九]][[zh:十一月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q840521]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 29|qq=November 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}. == Events == * [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:978|978]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]] ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Heian period)|Jōgen]] to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to station [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] and [[:en:Jitō|jitō]] throughout the provinces. == Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Takahisa]] , a military commander († [[:en:1604|1604]] ). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 19|January 19]] ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei Kimiko|1=Koganei Kimiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464316}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of [[:en:Mori Ōgai|Mori Ōgai]] († [[:en:1956|1956]] ).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 7th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1758|1758]] [[:en:January 9|January 9]] ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久留島義太|1=Kurushima Kinai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3915605}} , a wasan expert (* [[:en:1660|1660]] circa).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1129|qq=}}

day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:978|978]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]] ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Heian period)|Jōgen]] to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] first year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to station [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] and ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Takahisa]] , a military commander († [[:en:1604|1604]] ). * [[:en:Meiji
