<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿九]][[zh:十一月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿九]][[zh:十一月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿九]][[zh:十一月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q840521]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 29|qq=November 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}. == Events == * {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|WD=Q1327101|lt=Tengen}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|978|en|978|WD=Q30318|lt=978}} {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Heian period)|en|Jōgen (Heian period)|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}} {{ill|December 22|en|December 22|WD=Q2636|lt=December 22}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to station {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=shugo}} and {{ill|Jitō|en|Jitō|WD=Q1329805|lt=jitō}} throughout the provinces. == Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1546|en|1546|WD=Q6533|lt=1546}} {{ill|December 22|en|December 22|WD=Q2636|lt=December 22}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Takahisa}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|en|1604|WD=Q6738|lt=1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|January 19|en|January 19|WD=Q2266|lt=January 19}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei Kimiko|1=Koganei Kimiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464316}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|en|Mori Ōgai|WD=Q356960|lt=Mori Ōgai}} († {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1758|en|1758|WD=Q7639|lt=1758}} {{ill|January 9|en|January 9|WD=Q2238|lt=January 9}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久留島義太|1=Kurushima Kinai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3915605}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} circa).

{{Not to be confused with|November 29|qq=November 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 29''' is the 29th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}. == Events == * {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|WD=Q1327101|lt=Tengen}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|978|en|978|WD=Q30318|lt=978}} {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31}} ) – Because the following year was struck by the misfortune of Yōgo, the era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Heian period)|en|Jōgen (Heian period)|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen}} to {{ill|era change|ja|改元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era change|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}} * {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji}} first year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}} {{ill|December 22|en|December 22|WD=Q2636|lt=December 22}} ) – The Kamakura Shogunate, under the pretext of pursuing {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} , obtained imperial authorization from {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|lt=Go-Shirakawa Hōō|lt_ja=後白河法皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to station {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=shugo}} and {{ill|Jitō|en|Jitō|WD=Q1329805|lt=jitō}} throughout the provinces. == Births == * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1546|en|1546|WD=Q6533|lt=1546}} {{ill|December 22|en|December 22|WD=Q2636|lt=December 22}} ) – {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Takahisa}} , a military commander († {{ill|1604|en|1604|WD=Q6738|lt=1604}} ). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|January 19|en|January 19|WD=Q2266|lt=January 19}} ) – {{ill|Koganei Kimiko|ja|小金井喜美子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei Kimiko|1=Koganei Kimiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464316}} , a novelist, essayist, and younger sister of {{ill|Mori Ōgai|en|Mori Ōgai|WD=Q356960|lt=Mori Ōgai}} († {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1758|en|1758|WD=Q7639|lt=1758}} {{ill|January 9|en|January 9|WD=Q2238|lt=January 9}} ) – {{ill|Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja|久留島喜内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurushima Yoshinobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久留島義太|1=Kurushima Kinai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3915605}} , a wasan expert (* {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} circa).

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day; if {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second-last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
